title: Why Americans Still Work 40 Hours a Week
subtitle: 美国人为何工作这么拼

paragraphs.en: How will we all keep busy when we only have to work 15 hours a week? That was the question that worried the British economist John Maynard Keynes when he wrote his short essay 'Economic Possibilities for Our Grandchildren' in 1930. Over the next century, he predicted, the economy would become so productive that people would barely need to work at all. For a while, it looked like Keynes was right. In 1930 the average working week was 47 hours in the United States. But by 1970, the number of hours Americans worked on average had fallen to slightly less than 39.
paragraphs.zh: 若每周只需工作15小时，我们该如何保持忙碌？这正是英国经济学家约翰·梅纳德·凯恩斯1930年撰写短文《我们子孙的经济可能性》时担忧的问题。他预测，在未来一个世纪，经济将变得如此高效，人们几乎无需工作。有一段时间，凯恩斯似乎是对的。1930年美国平均每周工作47小时，但到1970年，美国人平均工作时长已降至略低于39小时。

paragraphs.en: But then something changed. Instead of continuing to decline, the duration of the working week remained stable. It has stayed at just below 40 hours for nearly five decades. So what happened? Why are people working just as much today as in 1970? There would be no mystery in this if Keynes had been wrong about the power of technology to increase the economy's productivity, which he thought would lead to a standard of living between four and eight times as high as it is today.
paragraphs.zh: 但后来情况变了。每周工作时长不再继续下降，而是保持稳定。近五十年来，它一直停留在略低于40小时的水平。究竟发生了什么？为何今天人们的工作时长与1970年相当？若凯恩斯对技术提升经济生产力的判断有误，这便不足为奇——他曾认为这将使生活水平达到“今天的四到八倍”。

paragraphs.en: But Keynes got that right: Technology has made the economy massively more productive. According to Benjamin Friedman, an economist at Harvard, the U.S. economy is right on track to reach Keynes's eight-fold multiple by 2029. That is a century after the last data Keynes would have had access to. In a new paper, Friedman tries to figure out why that increased productivity has not translated into increased leisure time. Perhaps people just never feel materially satisfied, always wanting more money to buy the next new thing. This is a theory that appeals to many economists.
paragraphs.zh: 但凯恩斯这点说对了：技术已使经济生产力大幅提升。据哈佛大学经济学家本杰明·弗里德曼称，美国经济正稳步迈向2029年达到凯恩斯所说八倍水平的目标——那正是凯恩斯所能获取的最新数据之后的一个世纪。在一篇新论文中，弗里德曼试图弄清为何生产力提高并未转化为更多休闲时间。也许人们永远无法在物质上感到满足，总想要更多钱购买下一件新东西。这一理论吸引了许多经济学家。

paragraphs.en: This argument is, at best, far from sufficient, he writes. If that were the case, why did the duration of the working week decline in the first place? Another theory Friedman considers is that, in an era of ever fewer settings that provide effective opportunities for personal connections and relationships, people may place more value on the socializing that happens at work. There is support for this theory. Many people today consider colleagues as friends. But Friedman argues that the evidence for this theory is far from conclusive. Many workers report that they would like to spend more time with family, rather than at work. Furthermore, this theory cannot explain the change in trend in the U.S. working week in the 1970s.
paragraphs.zh: 他写道：“这一论点充其量远不充分。”若果真如此，为何每周工作时长起初会下降？弗里德曼考虑的另一个理论是，在能有效提供人际联系与关系机会的环境日益减少的时代，人们可能更重视工作中发生的社交。这一理论有据可依。如今许多人视同事为朋友。但弗里德曼认为，支持该理论的证据远非确凿。许多员工表示更愿与家人而非同事共度时光。此外，该理论无法解释20世纪70年代美国工作周趋势的转变。

paragraphs.en: A third possibility proves more convincing for Friedman. That is: American inequality means that the gains of increasing productivity are not widely shared by everyone. In other words, most Americans are too poor to work less. Unlike the other two explanations Friedman considers, this one fits chronologically. Inequality declined in America during the period following World War II, along with the duration of the working week. But since the early 1970s it has risen dramatically.
paragraphs.zh: 第三种可能性对弗里德曼而言更具说服力。那就是：美国的不平等意味着生产力提高的收益并未被广泛共享。换言之，大多数美国人太穷，无法减少工作量。与弗里德曼考虑的前两种解释不同，这一解释在时间顺序上吻合。二战后美国不平等程度下降，每周工作时长也随之减少。但自20世纪70年代初以来，不平等程度急剧上升。

paragraphs.en: Keynes's prediction of a shorter working week rests on the idea that the standard of living would continue rising for everyone. But Friedman says that this is not what has happened. Although Keynes's eight-fold figure holds up for the economy as a whole, it is not at all the case for the median American worker. For them, output by 2029 is likely to be around 3.5 times what it was when Keynes was writing. This is a bit below his four- to eight-fold predicted range.
paragraphs.zh: 凯恩斯关于缩短工作周的预测建立在每个人生活水平持续提高的理念之上。但弗里德曼表示事实并非如此。尽管凯恩斯的八倍数字对整体经济而言成立，但对美国中位工人来说完全不是这回事。对他们而言，到2029年的产出可能仅为凯恩斯写作时的3.5倍左右，略低于他预测的四到八倍区间。

paragraphs.en: This can be seen in the median worker's income over this time period, complete with a shift in 1973 that fits in precisely with when the working week stopped shrinking. According to Friedman, between 1947 and 1973 the average hourly wage for normal workers (those who were not in management roles) in private industries other than agriculture nearly doubled in terms of what their money could buy. But by 2013 the average hourly wage for ordinary workers had fallen 5 percent from the 1973 level in terms of actual purchasing power. Thus, though American incomes may have gone up since 1973, the amount that American workers can actually buy with their money has gone down.
paragraphs.zh: 这从同期中位工人收入的变化中可见一斑，1973年出现的转折恰好与工作周停止缩短的时间吻合。据弗里德曼称，1947至1973年间，非农私营行业普通工人（非管理岗位）的平均时薪购买力几乎翻倍。但到2013年，普通工人的平均时薪实际购买力比1973年水平下降了5%。因此，尽管1973年以来美国人的名义收入可能上升，但工人实际能购买的东西却减少了。

paragraphs.en: For most Americans, then, the magic of increasing productivity stopped working around 1973. Thus, they had to keep working just as much in order to maintain their standard of living. What Keynes predicted was a very optimistic version of what economists call technological unemployment. This is the idea that less labor will be necessary because machines can do so much. In Keynes's vision, the resulting unemployment would be distributed more or less evenly across society in the form of increased leisure.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，对大多数美国人而言，生产力提高的魔力在1973年左右便失效了。他们不得不继续工作同样长的时间以维持生活水平。凯恩斯预测的是经济学家所称“技术性失业”的一个非常乐观的版本，即由于机器能做大量工作，所需人力将减少。在凯恩斯的设想中，由此产生的失业将以增加休闲的形式或多或少均匀分布于社会。

paragraphs.en: But Friedman says that, for Americans, reality is much darker. Americans now have a labor market in which millions of people — those with fewer skills and less education — are seeking whatever poorly paid work they can get. This is confirmed by a recent poll that found that, for half of hourly workers, their top concern is not that they work too much but that they work too little. This is most likely not because they like their jobs so much. Rather, we can assume it is because they need the money.
paragraphs.zh: 但弗里德曼表示，对美国而言现实要黑暗得多。如今美国的劳动力市场中，数以百万计技能较低、受教育较少的人正在寻找任何能获得的低薪工作。近期一项民调证实了这一点：半数时薪工人最担心的并非工作太多，而是工作太少。这很可能并非因为他们热爱工作，而是因为他们需要钱。

paragraphs.en: This explanation leaves an important question. If the very rich — the workers who have reaped above-average gains from the increased productivity since Keynes's time — can afford to work less, why do they continue to work so much? (Indeed, research has shown that the highest earners in America tend to work the most.) Friedman believes that for many top earners, work is a labor of love. They are doing work they care about and are interested in, and doing more of it is not necessarily a burden. For them, it may even be a pleasure. These top earners derive meaning from their jobs and work is an important part of how they think of themselves. And, of course, they are compensated for it at a level that makes it worth their while.
paragraphs.zh: 这一解释留下一个重要问题。如果非常富有的人——那些自凯恩斯时代以来从生产力提高中获得高于平均水平收益的劳动者——有能力减少工作，为何他们仍工作如此之多？（事实上，研究表明美国收入最高者往往工作最多。）弗里德曼认为，对许多高收入者而言，工作是出于热爱。他们在做自己关心和感兴趣的工作，多做并非负担，甚至可能是乐趣。这些高收入者从工作中获得意义，工作是他们自我认同的重要组成部分。当然，他们获得的报酬也使其值得付出。

paragraphs.en: Friedman concludes that the prosperity Keynes predicted is here. After all, the economy as a whole has grown even more brilliantly than he expected. But for most Americans, that prosperity is nowhere to be seen. And, as a result, neither are those shorter working weeks.
paragraphs.zh: 弗里德曼总结道，凯恩斯预言的繁荣已经到来。毕竟整体经济的增长甚至比他预期的更加辉煌。但对大多数美国人而言，那繁荣却不见踪影。结果，更短的工作周也同样遥不可及。
